FUNNELS

“Choreography”

Breakdown Of How To
Launch - AND RUN - A
$10MM+ ‘Evergreen’
Virtual Event!)
by RUSSELL BRUNSON | PG 4

How To Make
ANY Irresistible
Offer Relevant
For Solving
TODAY’S
Problems!
by HEATH WILCOCK | PG 10	Log In to the Members Area

-- 1 of 20 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 20 --

Dear Funnel Hackers,
At the last Funnel Hacking LIVE event, Tony Robbins said something that has stuck
with me over the past few months.
Good times make weak people.
Weak people make lousy times.
Lousy times make strong people.
Strong people make great times.
In those few words, he showed the cycle that we go through personally in our
own lives, but also the conditions of the marketplace.
Over the past decade, we have been in good times, but it made most people
weak. When the lockdowns started almost two years ago, it hit our society at a
time when it was weak, and it created lousy times for many.
From here, everyone progresses on a different timetable, but collectively it
is making people strong. I’ve watched the entrepreneurs I serve quickly pivot
and look for ways to create great times once again.
Prior to the 2008 crash, there were live events happening in my core industry
(and most of the industries of my entrepreneur friends) about three or four
times a week, almost every week of the year. When the crash hit, the events
business dried up. It took five or six years after the initial crash before I started
to see people willing to put their money back into running live events.
By 2020, events were once again happening every week. But when the
lockdown came, it halted those events even faster than the crash of 2008.
But this time, it didn’t take five or six years to get back. This time, because
of technology, I watched as within weeks our entrepreneurs quickly
shifted from live in-person events to virtual events.
FEB 2022
MEMBER SPOTLIGHT:
CAMILLE BECKSTRAND
by Camille Beckstrand | Pg. 13
VIRTUAL REAL ESTATE
SECRETS
by Heath Wilcock | Pg. 10

by Russell Brunson | Pg. 4

MAKE YOURSELF MORE
by Dan Kennedy | pg 14
SECRETS OF SUCCESS
ACHIEVEMENT VS.
FULFILLMENT
by Russell Brunson | Pg. 15
Featuring Russell Brunson
CEO of ClickFunnels Behind The Scenes Of Our Virtual Event
Funnel That Put 259 People Into Our
$25K Coaching Program In A Weekend!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 20 --

4 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
If it wasn’t for the lockdowns, I don’t think people
would have been willing to sit on Zoom and watch
a three-day virtual event, but because everyone was
in forced lockdown and bored, many were willing to
try it. And now I think a majority of people actually
prefer it this way. The good news is that because
of the lockdowns, we now have a new way to hold
events that was never possible before.
A few months back, I met with my highest-level
Inner Circle and Category Kings mastermind groups.
The common theme between most of my seven-,
eight-, and nine-figure entrepreneurs was that virtual
events had made them the most money within the
past 12 months. Everyone had their own take on
HOW to run a virtual event, what the funnels looked
like, and how they closed sales during the event, but
the big aha from our mastermind week was that we
all need to be doing MORE virtual events.
mADE THEM THE MOST MONEY-
WITHIN THE PAST 12 MONTHS.
This month, I wanted to show you behind the scenes of
our virtual event funnel. We launched it a few weeks
into the lockdowns, and it was amazing! But even
more powerful than the initial event launch was
the fact that we’ve now “evergreened” this funnel,
and we relaunch it every 90 days. It has single-hand-
edly done over eight figures in sales by itself!
Now to put this into perspective, let me show you
what it takes for us to put on Funnel Hacking LIVE
versus doing a virtual event.
Does this get you excited?? Does this make you want
to do a virtual event? Cool, then let me show you
behind the scenes of how our event funnel works.
THE INVISIBLE
VIRTUAL EVENT
FUNNEL
by RUSSELL BRUNSON
I’ll start this by giving you a really cool gift. My In-
ner Circle members pay $50K a year, and the rate
for my Category Kings group is $150K a year. The
things we talk about in those groups are private,
and I rarely if ever share them outside of the
room. But I wanted to share this one video with
you from the mastermind. It’s a quick 10-minute
breakdown I gave those groups about this funnel.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 20 --

5	FEBRUARY LE T TER
STEP #1:
THE INVISIBLE FUNNEL
The invisible funnel is a concept that was originally
created by Daegan Smith, and I shared it in the first
edition of the DotComSecrets book. It’s a concept
I’ve used a few dozen times, but for some reason I
can never get anyone else to do it. Because so few
people ever use it, I actually pulled it out of the up-
dated version of the book. But it still works (as you’ll
see here) when used properly.
So the concept of the invisible funnel is that you
have people sign up for a premium paid training,
but they don’t have to pay for the event until AFTER
it’s over, and only IF they love it. So they put in
their credit card details and attend the event. If
they decide afterward that they didn’t get value and
don’t want to pay for it, they can let us know, and we
won’t bill them anything.
This takes away all the risk for them, and it
dramatically increases your sign-up rate for a paid
event. My guess is I get double to triple as many
people to sign up, yet only about 10% of the people
ask to not get billed. So the net increase in sign-ups
is pretty dramatic.
Here is the landing page so you can see how we
position the event. (You can see the most current
version at TwoCommaClubLIVE.com.)
You’ll notice that the big idea/hook/angle (whatever
you’d like to call it) is that this event will help them
build out a personal roadmap so they can win a Two
Comma Club award. We also focus on the fact that
they can attend from the comfort of their own
home, and that they only have to pay for it AFTER
the ev ent is over, if they like it. All of those hooks
are set above the fold on the page.
Take a minute to watch the actual video sales letter
for this event, so you can see how the copy pitches
the event, as well as how the invisible funnel works.
(We had SO MUCH FUN putting together this video
as a family!)
To watch this video, scan
the QR code or go to
www.TwoCommaClubLive.com
To watch this video, scan
the QR code or go to
www.InnerCircleForLife.com/virtualevent10min
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 20 --

6 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS
Under the video, we have a long-form sales page that
matches what is mentioned in the video for those
whose core learning modality is the written word.
The key to an invisible funnel is to be able to explain
HOW the game is played. Above the order form, we
have this block that shows them how it works.
Under the “This Is How It Works” block, we add in
two ways they can sign up. The first is the invisible
funnel as it’s explained in the copy above. But the
second, which we label “most popular,” has a $50
discount AND access to the replays if they prepay
right then. You can see how we structured this offer
and wrote the copy here.
This is the breakdown showing what options
people choose.
• 57.45% Invisible Funnel $0 – $197
• 42.55% Prepay for $97
After that, we ask for their credit card info, and then
right before the order button, we insert an order form
bump. As you know from past issues, the bump is the
closest thing to free money you’ll ever see. Often-
times, this bump will cover all of your ad costs.
There should NEVER be a funnel without an order
form bump on it…unless of course, you hate money!
Our conversion rate on this bump is 10.13% ($97).
STEP #2:
THE VIP UPSELL
The next step is offering a VIP Upsell where people
can become VIPs. We charged $197 for this upgrade
that allows them to have private Q&A with our
coaches between breaks and during lunch. Our con-
version rates on this step were 10.05% OTO 1 ($197),
but even MORE fascinating was that most of our
$25K buyers on Day 2 came from this group of VIPs.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 20 --

7	FEBRUARY LE T TER
They will self-select so you’ll know who the action-takers are!
After that, we take them to the thank-you page, where they get more
information on how to attend the event.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 20 --

8 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
HOW TO STRUCTURE YOUR EVENTThe next question I get a lot is “How do I structure my event?”
I doodled out the basic structure that we use for our virtual events here.
I am a BIG believer in doing virtual events over three days. For those who have studied
my Perfect Webinar framework, you’ll notice that the structure of how you teach is almost
identical to what we do in a 90-minute webinar, but we do it over three days. (You can get
this framework for $7 at www.PerfectWebinarSecrets.com.)
DAY #1
We start off the event by teaching them the strategy behind the new opportunity (or vehicle).
You are giving them a new way to get success or a certain result that they want in some aspect
of their life. This is where I would try to teach the strategy behind funnels, and you’d teach the
strategy behind what you do. My goal for this session is for them to say, “Ahhh—I need a funnel!”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 20 --

9	FEBRUARY LE T TER
The second part of the day is all about breaking
their own internal false beliefs that say that they
can’t do it. I will often workshop out the process—
what they are doing, and why. As I teach this
session, my goal is to have them say, “Yes, I think
that I could actually build a funnel!”
DAY #2
Now they are sold on the fact that they need a
funnel, and they believe that they could actually
do it! On Day 2, I want to talk about the external
false beliefs that they believe would keep them
from having success. The external fear many people
have is traffic, as in not having enough people getting
to their funnels. So my goal for this session is for
them to say, “Not only do I believe in funnels and
know that I can build one myself, I also believe that I
FINALLY know how to drive traffic to it!”
The next session is where I pitch my actual
product or service. If you’ve ever seen me sell
coaching at Funnel Hacking LIVE or on our virtual
event, Two Comma Club LIVE, you’ll notice that
this presentation never introduces them to new
strategies. Instead, it’s usually me talking about
who they have to become if they’re going to have
success with this new opportunity I introduced
them to. I focus more on personal development
than on new strategies.
After I teach things to help them get out of their
heads so they’ll be able to do the work, then I
present my stack and the close. Once again, I don’t
have the time or space in this newsletter to explain
and show the full stack and close, but I talk about
them in-depth in the Perfect Webinar Secrets $7
product as well as in the Expert Secrets book.
After the pitch, I’ll normally do a break. Then at
the end of the day, I come back and show success
stories from people who have already been in the
coaching program. I can do this by having a round-
table where I interview my coaching students, or
by sharing case studies of their success.
DAY #3
Here I want to go deeper into personal development.
My intro presentation is normally more of a “You can
do it!” style presentation, where my goal at the end is
for them to feel like they could accomplish anything.
The second presentation is called a “re-pitch,” where
I re-explain the offer I made the day before and give
them one last shot at purchasing. After I make the last
call to action, we do a 30-minute break and put up a
countdown clock to when the offer closes.
After the break, I spend the rest of the day serving
them at my highest level. I teach them whatever
I think will help the most. Oftentimes, it’s a lot of
personal development advice to help them leave the
event feeling like they could accomplish anything!
I know that was a pretty fast run-through of how
we structure the event. But hopefully it will help
you realize how we choreograph every minute of
the process to maximize sales at the end. Just doing a
virtual event will not necessarily make you any money,
unless you take the attendees on a journey that will…
• Break the false beliefs holding them back in life.
• Give them a new framework to achieve the
success they desire most.
• Make an offer that is truly irresistable
• Help them to believe in themselves.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 20 --

10 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
VIRTUAL REAL
ESTATE SECRETS
A Monthly Bonus Behind-The-Scenes
Look At The Marketing Sorcery We’re
Using In Our Ecomm Funnels That You
Can Steal And Profit From...Big-Time!
by HEATH WILCOCK
Hey, it’s me, your fellow Heathykins.
Let’s get right to it, because I’m hopped up on all
sorts of nootropics, caffeine, and Body By Brunson®
Essence that you spray under your nose and it
makes you talk fast and not sleep.
SO HERE WE GOOOOOOOOO!
To piggyback off of January 2022’s Virtual Real
Estate Secrets article, where I talked about the
importance of making your offer new, I now want
to talk about...
How To Make Your
Offer Relevant For
Solving
TODAY’S
Problems!
One of my favorite things to do is to go hunting for
old ads at local antique shops.
Here’s an ad from 1965 I recently found.
Imagine that...
An ad that promotes the positives of what many
would demonize today—sugar. (Side note: If you
do a quick image search in Google for “sugar ads,”
you’ll find more of these campaigns. Have a look.
They’re awesome.)
Can you picture an ad promoting the positive
benefits of sugar in today’s time?
Absolutely not. Now, the ad isn’t “wrong” per se.
Sugar does in fact give you quick energy.
However, in today’s time, it’s no longer seen as a
relevant solution to solving low energy, and in many
cases, is now considered to be a toxic culprit of all
sorts of health issues, including energy crashes.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 20 --

11	FEBRUARY LE T TER
With that said, I want to take a closer look at this sugar
ad and show what it’s doing really well, and how you
can use the techniques here for your own offer.
The attention-grabbing image of the kid climbing a
rope illustrates the overall theme of this ad, which
is “energy.” This is a positive, future-pacing image
showing how sugar is giving Billy the energy he
needs to climb.
(If I had been an ad man in the 1960s and I was
tasked to do another sugar ad, I would show a line
of kids all struggling to climb, only getting a few
feet off the ground...and then I would have one
kid already at the top. The headline would say
something like: “Can You Tell Which Mom Made
Sure Her Kid Got His Sugar?” Oh, the 60s...what
a time to be in marketing. Sell sugar on the
front end, cigarettes on the back.)
The first line of the ad “The name of the game is
energy” comes out and states the angle right away:
to have energy. It doesn’t talk about the sweetness
or how sugar makes everything taste better.
It focuses on what moms WANT, which is to
make sure their kids have fast-acting energy,
something they wouldn’t get from artificial
sweeteners, which you can see was their
competition, and still is to this day.
It’s a short story ad. It doesn’t need to be
dramatic like what you may see with a lot of
story-based sales copy today. It also doesn’t
need to go on and on for pages.
The story of Billy is simple—he’s always moving,
and even when he’s asleep, he’s growing. When
he needs energy, he gets it from sugar. That’s it.
You get a snapshot of a “Day With Billy” and what
his mom does to make sure he’s still going strong.
The “problem mechanism,” which in this ad
is exhaustion. What I like is how it doesn’t just
stop at exhaustion, but goes deeper and explains
what happens when exhaustion isn’t treated
right away.
Take a look:
It says, “Exhaustion opens the door a little wider
to the bugs and ailments that are always lying in
wait.” This is a great way of adding urgency with-
out saying, “Get it while it lasts!” by highlighting
what could happen if exhaustion isn’t treated,
and right away—it will only become worse.
Lastly, the ad states the main difference between
sugar and artificial sweetener, saying how “sugar
puts back energy” when “synthetic sweeteners”
put back nothing. (I also love the use of “synthetic,”
which sounds more ominous compared to the easy-
sounding “sugar.”)
The point of all this?
I like breaking down and analyzing old ads,
and showing how even though the offer
may not be relevant for today’s time, the
copy, strategy, and message can easily be
transferred to another offer.
•
•
•
•
•
•
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 20 --

12 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
To show how time changes our perception of
a solution. In other words, sugar was used to
solve the problem of children lacking energy in
the 1960s when “synthetic sweeteners” were
being introduced. The problem of low en-
ergy will NEVER go away, but the solution will
change based on your customers’ current-day
problems.
For example, we’re currently working on a new sleep
supplement offer called Blackout. Take a look.
For this offer, I did an enormous amount of research,
including getting on the phone with early users of
the product to get feedback on their experience.
And I noticed an interesting trend that no other
sleep offer out there was addressing, which was a
new, relevant problem in today’s time called “sleep
procrastination.”
Sleep procrastination is a new phenomenon where
people stay up suuuuuuuper late because they
DON’T WANT to go to sleep for whatever reason.
Oftentimes, it’s because they don’t want to wake up
and “start it all over again” with perhaps a job they
don’t like.
They struggle to “shut down their brain”
and have the DESIRE to go to sleep.
So what do they do?
They lie awake in their bed and scroll through their
phone on Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube, firing off
dopamine and keeping them glued to their phone as
a way to “push away” the next day’s agenda.
The sleep supplement space is very saturated. And
everyone knows they need sleep. What we did for
Blackout was to help create the desire for sleep,
and in turn, help the customer WANT to put down
their phone, not procrastinate, and start snoozing
right away.
When creating your offer, consider these two things:
What current-day problem does my offer solve?
How does my offer solve my customers’
problems better than other offers out there?
(Remember, “sugar puts back energy, when
synthetic sweeteners put nothing back.”)
Okay, that’s it for this month’s VRE Secrets.
I hope you were able to get some value out of it and
see how you may implement it for your own offer to
get some killer results.
Until next month, sweetums.
Your friend,
Heath Wilcock
•
•
•
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 20 --

13	FEBRUARY LE T TER
MEMBER
SPOTLIGHT:
CAMILLE
BECKSTRAND
by CAMILLE BECKSTRAND
My sisters and I think of ourselves as “unexpected
entrepreneurs.” Ten years ago, we started a small
blog to stay in touch, and it snowballed into one of
the largest recipe websites in the United States. We
found our little hobby blog had turned into a full-
fledged business in a very short amount of time.
Over the past 10 years, we have showed up every
day, offering new recipes to our followers on multiple
social media platforms. Our recipes are completely
free, but we earn money from page views that
we generate from the various platforms. This has
become quite the little business. We were grateful
for the small success we had seen, but we knew that
there was MORE.
We came across Russell Brunson’s podcast and
started deep-diving into his content. There was one
episode that we listened to where he mentioned
that “serving is selling and selling is serving.” Before
hearing this phrase, I had always thought of selling
as slimy and cold-hearted because I had the mindset
that selling was all about taking people’s money.
But as Russell explained further, selling is how we
provide people more value. And people are search-
ing for anything of value that makes their lives better.
Across all of our social media channels, we have a
combined audience of four million followers. We
thought that we had been providing them what they
wanted by giving them free recipes, but little did we
realize that we were missing the boat! Nervously, we
started small and put together some simple digital
meal plans that we sold as PDF files. We didn’t know
how it would be received, but we focused on “serv-
ing is selling.”
Our small digital meal plan EXPLODED. Most of our
followers wanted that additional value and were
willing to pay for it because it made their lives easier.
That small shift in our mindset completely changed
our company, and we more than doubled our compa-
ny’s revenue in one year. Our “little hobby blog” now
provided more than a full-time income for myself,
each of my sisters, and our mom, plus a handful of
part-time contractors.
This year, we invested in the 2CCX Coaching Program
because while we have seen great success in our
business, we know that there is MORE. We have only
scratched the surface of providing our followers the
value they need.
The coaches in the 2CCX program have analyzed our
company and helped us hone in on our goals and
the outcomes we want in our business, which has
completely changed our marketing strategy. They
are helping us be comfortable with being uncomfort-
able and trying new things that we had never even
dreamed about, which is pushing us to level up and
become the kind of business owners we have been
called to be.
2022 is going to be our biggest and best year yet and
it is because we recognize that the best way to serve
your audience is to sell to them.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 20 --

14 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
MAGNETIC
MARKETING
WITH
DAN KENNEDY
If You Want More, Make
Yourself More Valuable
The mayor of a small town once wrote to Benjamin
Franklin asking for a donation, so the town could buy
a bell for its town square. Franklin sent money with
a note suggesting they forego the bell in favor of
buying books for the town library. It is at the library
we might find an answer to why so few succeed and
why most fail—at anything, at everything.
Most people do not apply themselves to acquiring
know-how nor do they apply the know-how they
acquire. In short, they have the attention span of a
gnat, the diligence of an idle, random breeze. They
certainly don’t study.
I have become quite rich and somewhat celebrated,
reaching the pinnacle of success in not one but three
different fields. At each required skill set, I once
sucked. For me, there has always been a crawl to
competence, then a fast rise to superiority. Part of
the process is getting through a lot of information in
a hurry but also continuously.
For nearly 25 years, I read a book a day plus news-
papers, trade journals, and newsletters. I visited the
public library weekly and always took on a needed
skill so thoroughly and intensely, studying it to be-
come a world-class expert.
When I was teaching myself to be an advertising
copywriter, for example, I studied no less than an
hour every day and listened to recorded material on
the subject constantly. I sought out and got to know
the top people in the field. And when one told me to
take great direct-response ads and write them out
longhand 21 times each to teach my subconscious
the rhythm of such writing, I did that with 100 ads.
I collected over 200 books on the subject and
immersed myself in them. I built organized files of
samples that fill a room. I traced one master back to
his teachers and them to theirs, thus even knowing
the genealogy of the field.
When I am asked by fledgling or journeymen copy-
writers how they too might have clients waiting in
line to pay them million-dollar fees when there are
thousands of copywriters advertising their avail-
ability for a tenth of that or less, and I tell them this
answer, they reject it. They seek rewards that are
out of kilter with their value and are unwilling to do
what is necessary to build up their value.
The same answer could be given by the top earners
in insurance, real estate, retail store ownership,
dentistry—name the business or profession. The
answer is the same.
I am told by people all the time that they simply do
not have time to read and listen to all the material
they have purchased or subscribed to. But time is
democratic and just. Everyone has the same amount.
When I choose to read during my midmorning
coffee break, and you choose to blather about trivia
with friends… When I choose to study for an hour
sitting on my backyard deck at day’s end, but you
choose to watch a TiVo’d American Idol episode…
When someone says he does not have the time to
apply himself to acquiring the know-how required to
create sufficient value for his stated desires, he is a
farmer surrounded by ripe fruit and vegetables, whole
grains, and a herd of cattle on his own property
who dies of starvation, unable to organize his time
and discipline himself to eat.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 20 --

15	FEBRUARY LE T TER
Incidentally, success in every business, including
yours, depends on mastery of a handful of critical
competencies (one of which is always marketing).
The individual who sets out earnestly and diligently
to acquire a wealth of know-how in each winds up
with wealth in his bank account. All others watch
with envy and cry in their soup, two activities they
do seem to find time for.
SECRETS OF
SUCCESS
by R U S S E L L B R U N S O N
ACHIEVEMENT VS.
FULFILLMENT
The invitation said I needed to be at the Jackson Hole
airport on August 23 before 2:00 pm. I didn’t know
much of what was going to happen, other than that
the invite had come from Brendon Burchard, author
of High Performance Habits. He said everyone he had
invited was an A Player and that I needed to be there.
When I got off the plane in Jackson Hole, Wyoming,
and walked into the small airport, I started to recognize
some of the other people who were also walking toward
the baggage claim. I had seen many of them on video
and listened to all of their podcasts, and almost every-
one I saw had been on the New York Times Best Seller
list at least once! What reality was I experiencing?!
We picked up our bags and drove to a nearby airfield,
where a half dozen private helicopters waited for us!
As I climbed into the helicopter I was assigned to, I
noticed that sitting next to me was Tom Bilyeu, who
had recently sold his company, Quest Nutrition,
for a billion dollars! Across from me was Dean
Graziosi, who had been on TV every day for almost
two decades selling his books.
Over the next 72 hours, we fished, shot guns,
raced kayaks, and masterminded in the beautiful
mountains while sitting around a campfire. The
experience was unreal. I remember going home
from that trip wondering if I’d ever experience
something like that again.
One year later, we met again as a group, this time
in Puerto Rico with our spouses. This event had the
same people as our trip to Jackson Hole, but the
experience was so much different. We spent a few
days getting pampered at the spa, hiking together in
the mountains, and swimming in the rivers. To top it
off, we spent three days masterminding on how we
could each change the world in our own little way. I
remember sitting around that table pinching myself
because every moment felt like a dream.
At the end of that meeting, we decided to make it
official. This was now a group of world-changers,
and we wanted to continue to meet each year to
hold each other accountable and give each other
inspiration and guidance.
About 12 months later, we returned as a group to
Puerto Rico where the magic had happened before.
During this third meeting, I was given one of the
greatest keys in my journey to discover the balance
between achievement and fulfillment.
About a dozen of us sat around a large table over-
looking the ocean. We had just finished a two-hour
mastermind session, and Craig Clemens asked us
if we all wanted to do something crazy, like skinny-
dipping in the ocean that night.
Wait…what? I thought. Did I hear him correctly?
We all stopped what we were doing and looked at
Craig to see if he was joking and to see if this was
something we were actually supposed to entertain.
He smiled, and then he asked us a question. “Have
you ever noticed that throughout your life, each year
seems to get shorter and shorter?”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 20 --

16 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
“Yeah,” we collectively agreed. “Time seems to fly by.”
Craig then went on to explain that while time isn't
actually going faster, it feels like it’s going faster
because our brain compresses and deletes things it
doesn’t think it needs to remember.
“For example,” he continued, “each of us is a hyper-
achiever. Most achievers live very structured lives.
We have morning routines and work routines to get
the most done in the shortest period of time. We
set up routines to help us lose weight, increase our
energy, and sleep better at night.”
We found ourselves nodding in agreement, thinking
of all the routines in our lives. He then explained that
while creating routines is one of the keys to success
and achievement, your brain has no reason to keep a
memory of the daily routines that repeat themselves
daily for weeks, months, or years at a time. Your brain
sees that it’s identical, so it deletes those memories.
In short, your days literally feel shorter.
Craig continued, “If we want to remember what we’ve
done here during this mastermind, we need to create
an event horizon.”
A what? I thought. I had never heard of this event
horizon concept.
Seeing our confused faces, Craig clarified, “Let me
explain. This is our third meeting together as a group,
right?” We all nodded.
Then he described each of our three meetings
together.
The first time, we met in Wyoming, where we
flew helicopters, shot guns, and rode horses. It
was such a unique experience that I will never
forget it. In fact, I can remember my life before
that experience, and after it. That was an event
horizon.
The next year, we came to Puerto Rico, and we
had another amazing, life-changing experience.
I have vivid memories of hiking in the mountains,
swimming in the river, and holding masterminds
late into the night. This was another event horizon.
But this year, we’re back in Puerto Rico—at the
same table, doing much of the same things we did
last time. So while it’s been amazing, our minds
will most likely delete most of this experience
because it’s so similar to our last trip.
And then he repeated his crazy idea. “We need to
create an event horizon right here, right now, so
we never forget this experience! We should all go
skinny-dipping tonight, or do something else crazy to
lock this experience into our memories forever!”
Now, I’m sure you are wondering whether we actu-
ally went skinny-dipping together as a group, and the
answer is...no. But we did end up having some other
amazing activities to help lock in the experience.
After the mastermind was over, I started thinking
more about these two opposite and seemingly
conflicting concepts of achievement and fulfill-
ment. Clearly, creating routines is one of the keys
to achievement. In fact, my routines are one of
the reasons I’ve had success in wrestling and in
my marriage, family, faith, and business. But if I’m
honest, while my routines helped drive achieve-
ment, they didn’t create fulfillment for me.
My fulfillment came at the end of a routine. I don’t
remember the hundreds of wrestling practices I did
in high school. I don’t remember the 10,000 shots
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 20 --

17	FEBRUARY LE T TER
I would take each year in practice preparing for
my matches. Every practice’s routine helped me to
achieve my goals, but the short six minutes of the
match is what gave me fulfillment. Winning my state
title and getting my hand raised—that I will never
forget. To this day, I still remember the sights, the
sounds, the smells, and the feelings.
Over the past 20 years of my life, I started to realize
that I was so focused on achievement that my rou-
tines became flawless and, as Craig had explained, it
seemed that my pursuit of achievement was actually
shortening my life. I found myself putting less and
less focus on the event horizons, oftentimes not
even taking time to celebrate the wins.
For me, I think it was easier to feel the wins in wres-
tling, because there was an actual referee who lifted
my hand after the match was over, letting me and
everyone watching know that I had reached my goal.
But there isn’t a referee lifting our hands to tell us
that we can enjoy the fruits of our labor, so even
when the small victories come, we quickly dive back
into routine searching for the next hit of success that
we crave so much.
THE YIN AND YANG
OF ACHIEVEMENT
AND FULFILLMENT
This was the first time I realized that achievement
and fulfillment weren’t connected. In fact, in many
ways they were opposites. And the very things I was
doing to have success were the same things that
oftentimes kept me from feeling fulfilled.
It wasn’t just routine versus event horizons.
Although after becoming aware of that concept,
I’m more aware of creating event horizons for
myself and for the people I’ve been called to
serve—my family, people in my church, and my
entrepreneurs.
I also started to see this yin and yang in dozens of
other areas in my life. As I became aware of them
and how they affected me, I was able to consciously
make changes that allowed me to continue striving
for success while still enjoying the fulfillment that
can come from the journey.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 20 --

18 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
